summary: A Go-based monolithic tool leveraging MCP for handling migrations, likely translating Goss configurations with an automated agent-driven testing pipeline.
concerns: Fragmented test generation via multiple glm-agents may lead to inconsistent test coverage
Deeply nested directory structure for agent file outputs (GOrchestra/glm-agents/...)
Potential duplication between baseline implementations in different agent directories
suggestions: Consolidate agent-generated test files into the main test suites to reduce fragmentation
Clean up or archive the GOrchestra agent output directories post-merge
Focus on core runner and MCP handler logic as they represent the primary business value
